The '''''Power of the Jedi Sourcebook''''' is a sourcebook for the ''[[Star Wars Roleplaying Game]]'' published by [[Wizards of the Coast]] on [[August 7]], [[2002]]. It extensively details the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[New Jedi Order]], including such details as beliefs, history, structure and views on the nature of [[The Force/Legends|the Force]].

==Contents==
===Chapter 1: History of the Force===
===Chapter 2: Playing in the Light===
*New Force Skills and Techniques
*New Force Feats
*New Prestige Classes

===Chapter 3: Gamemastering a Jedi Campaign===

===Chapter 7: Where the Force is Strong===
This chapter examines several sites associated with the Jedi.

*[[Chu'unthor]]
*[[Great Jedi Library/Legends|Great Jedi Library at Ossus]]
*[[Jedi Praxeum|Jedi Academy on Yavin 4]]
*[[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]]
*[[Meditative Canyon]] on Ambria
*[[The Oracle (Pelgrin)|The Oracle at Pelgrin]]
*[[School of Hidden Wisdom]]
*[[Valley of the Jedi]]

==Continuity==
The eighth page of the book erroneously states that [[Cay Qel-Droma]] killed [[Satal Keto/Legends|Satal Keto]],<ref name="Page 8">''Power of the Jedi Sourcebook'', page 8</ref> when the [[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 5|original comic]] establishes that his brother, [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]], killed Keto.<ref name="DLOTS5">[[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 5|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 5]]</ref>

Page nine of the book mentions that the Brotherhood of Darkness was formed around 2000 BBY by a rogue Jedi Knight. It further states that [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Skere Kaan|Kaan]] was simply the latest ruler of the organization.<ref name="Page 9">''Power of the Jedi Sourcebook'', page 9</ref> This contradicts other sources that establish Lord Kaan as the Brotherhood's founder.<ref name="EGF">[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']]</ref> [[Darth Ruin/Legends|Darth Ruin]] founded an [[New Sith|empire]] in 2000 BBY according to a number of sources, and [[2009]]'s ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' established that what remained of Ruin's Sith collapsed with the Republic in [[1100 BBY/Legends|1100 BBY]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> The page also mentions that the Jedi built the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] sometime after the [[Seventh Battle of Ruusan]],<ref name="Page 9" /> but ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' and ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' later established that there was a temple on Coruscant prior to that time.

The book indirectly references [[Tyvokka/Legends|Tyvokka]], but states he was killed in the [[Fifth Battle of Qotile]].

[[Saesee Tiin/Legends|Saesee Tiin's]] entry states he was killed during the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|First Battle of Geonosis]]; however, he survives until the [[Great Jedi Purge]] begins.

On page 117 of the book, under [[Even Piell/Legends|Even Piell's]] description, the text mentions that [[Darth Krayt|A'Sharad Hett]] is Piell's Padawan during the [[Mission to Malastare (Jedi High Council)|Malastare mission]]. However, by the comic book [[Star Wars: Emissaries to Malastare|''Emissaries to Malastare'']], Hett is [[Ki-Adi-Mundi/Legends|Ki-adi-Mundi's]] Padawan.
